description: prune a falsified node and its subtree, recording the reason as a negative constraint (the Decide step)
rules: marks the node and all descendants pruned (skipping merged/root nodes)
the REASON is stored as prune_reason and surfaces in Observe as a negative constraint
pruned-with-a-reason is more valuable than pruned-and-forgotten — failures are constraints, not noise
usage: Set NODE (the falsified node) and optional REASON (why this direction is a dead end). Output: prune.txt. If no run exists, a bootstrap run with a root + one child node (n1) is created first.
limitation: Marks state only — it does not delete artifacts, branches, or worktrees. A merged node or the root is never pruned. NODE must reference an existing node id.

vars.REASON: search-augmented judge overfits dev questions; no test transfer
